tool_input.plan:
# Plan: PulseBoard Channel Plugin for OpenClaw

## Context

OpenClaw uses a bundled workspace plugin architecture where each channel lives under `extensions/<id>/`. A new "PulseBoard" fictional channel plugin must be created following the same patterns as IRC, Nostr, and Telegram — using the Plugin SDK exclusively, never importing core internals. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform that supports direct messages and group "boards", with a 4000-character text chunk limit.

**Constraint:** Do NOT modify any existing files. Only create new files under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

---

## Critical Reference Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json` | Manifest format |
| `extensions/irc/package.json` | Package + `openclaw` metadata block |
| `extensions/irc/index.ts` | Entry point pattern |
| `extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts` | Setup-only entry pattern |
| `extensions/irc/api.ts` | Public API barrel |
| `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore` |
| `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts` | Internal SDK re-export barrel |
| `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` | Core `createChatChannelPlugin()` usage |
| `extensions/irc/src/types.ts` | `ResolvedAccount`, config type definitions |
| `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts` | `resolveAccount`, `listAccountIds` pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema` |
| `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts` | `ChannelSetupAdapter`, config patch helpers |
| `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts` | `ChannelSetupWizard` interactive UI |
| `extensions/irc/src/send.ts` | Outbound `sendText` implementation |
| `extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts` | Target normalization + allow-entry helpers |
| `extensions/irc/src/channel.test.ts` | Plugin test pattern |
| `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts` | Naming invariants to satisfy |

---

## SDK Entrypoints Used (all via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`)

- `core` — `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`
- `runtime-store` — `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `account-helpers` — `describeAccountSnapshot`, `createAccountListHelpers`
- `account-id` — `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId`
- `account-resolution` — `resolveMergedAccountConfig`
- `allow-from` — `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries`
- `channel-config-helpers` — `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, `adaptScopedAccountAccessor`, `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`
- `channel-config-schema` — `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, `ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape`
- `channel-policy` — `createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector`, `composeAccountWarningCollectors`
- `channel-setup` — `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupInput`
- `config-runtime` — `DmPolicy`
- `directory-runtime` — `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`, `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`
- `extension-shared` — `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`
- `routing` — `normalizeAccountId` (for setup)
- `secret-input` — `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`
- `setup` — `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `createAllowFromSection`, `ChannelSetupWizard`, `WizardPrompter`, `formatDocsLink`, `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`, `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`, `patchScopedAccountConfig`, `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`, `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `createSetupInputPresenceValidator`, `createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount`
- `status-helpers` — `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`
- `zod` — `z`

---

## Implementation Plan

### Directory Structure

```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json       # Plugin manifest
├── package.json               # npm package + openclaw metadata
├── index.ts                   # Full runtime entry point
├── setup-entry.ts             # Setup-only entry point
├── api.ts                     # Public API barrel
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts             # Core plugin (createChatChannelPlugin)
    ├── runtime.ts             # Runtime store
    ├── runtime-api.ts         # Internal SDK re-export barrel
    ├── types.ts               # TypeScript types (ResolvedAccount, config)
    ├── accounts.ts            # Account resolution helpers
    ├── config-schema.ts       # Zod config schema
    ├── normalize.ts           # Target normalization helpers
    ├── send.ts                # Outbound message sending stub
    ├── setup-core.ts          # ChannelSetupAdapter + config patch helpers
    ├── setup-surface.ts       # ChannelSetupWizard interactive UI
    └── channel.test.ts        # Plugin structure tests
```

---

### Step 1: `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`

Minimal manifest matching IRC pattern. `id` = `"pulseboard"`, `channels` = `["pulseboard"]`.

```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### Step 2: `extensions/pulseboard/package.json`

`name` = `"@openclaw/pulseboard"` (satisfies `@openclaw/<id>` naming invariant). `openclaw.channel.id` must equal `"pulseboard"` (same as plugin id). `openclaw.install.npmSpec` must equal `"@openclaw/pulseboard"`. No external runtime dependencies needed (PulseBoard API is a stub).

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "devDependencies": {
    "openclaw": "workspace:*"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "pulseboard",
      "label": "PulseBoard",
      "selectionLabel": "PulseBoard (Boards + DMs)",
      "detailLabel": "PulseBoard",
      "docsPath": "/channels/pulseboard",
      "docsLabel": "pulseboard",
      "blurb": "real-time bulletin board platform with named boards and direct messaging.",
      "aliases": ["pulse-board"]
    }
  }
}
```

---

### Step 3: `extensions/pulseboard/src/types.ts`

Define the config shape and `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`. Mirrors IRC `types.ts` structure.

Key types:
- `PulseBoardAccountConfig` — `apiUrl?`, `token?`, `username?`, `dmPolicy?`, `allowFrom?`, `defaultTo?`, `groupPolicy?`, `boards?`, `textChunkLimit?`
- `PulseBoardConfig` — extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig` with `accounts?` and `defaultAccount?`
- `CoreConfig` — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig } }`
- `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` — flat resolved shape with `accountId`, `enabled`, `configured` (bool), `apiUrl`, `token`, `username`, `config`
- `PulseBoardProbe` — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { apiUrl: string; username: string; latencyMs?: number }`
- `PulseBoardInboundMessage` — `{ messageId, boardId, senderId, senderUsername, text, timestamp, isBoard }`

---

### Step 4: `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime-api.ts`

Internal SDK barrel re-exporting the generic SDK helpers needed by `channel.ts`, `send.ts`, `inbound` logic. Since there is no `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` SDK subpath yet, import directly from generic SDK paths:

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the bundled PulseBoard extension.
export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";   // <-- generic helpers exposed via irc SDK surface
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  OpenClawConfig,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
```

> **Note:** The `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` SDK surface exposes truly generic helpers (`getChatChannelMeta`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`, etc.) that are suitable for any channel plugin. IRC uses them too — they're not IRC-specific despite the path name.

---

### Step 5: `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts`

Thin runtime store. Identical pattern to IRC.

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseBoardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");
export { getPulseBoardRuntime, setPulseBoardRuntime };
export function clearPulseBoardRuntime() {
  setPulseBoardRuntime(undefined as unknown as PluginRuntime);
}
```

---

### Step 6: `extensions/pulseboard/src/accounts.ts`

Account resolution. Uses `createAccountListHelpers` + `resolveMergedAccountConfig`. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`.

Exported:
- `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` (type)
- `listPulseBoardAccountIds(cfg)`
- `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId(cfg)`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })`
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg)`

Token resolution order: env `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN` (for default account) → `config.token` field.
API URL resolution: env `PULSEBOARD_API_URL` → config → default `https://api.pulseboard.example`
`configured` = `Boolean(apiUrl && token)`

---

### Step 7: `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`

Target normalization for PulseBoard. Boards are prefixed with `#` (e.g. `#announcements`), direct message targets are usernames.

Exported:
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — returns true if starts with `#`
- `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget(raw: string): string | undefined` — trims, strips `pulseboard:` prefix, handles `board:` prefix (adds `#`)
- `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(raw: string): boolean` — non-empty, no spaces, simple alphanumeric/`-`/`_`/`#` check
- `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(raw: string): string` — lowercase trim, strip `pulseboard:`/`user:` prefixes

---

### Step 8: `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts`

Zod schema using `buildChannelConfigSchema` + SDK schema helpers. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`.

```typescript
import {
  buildChannelConfigSchema,
  DmPolicySchema,
  GroupPolicySchema,
  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";

const PulseBoardBoardSchema = z.object({
  requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
  allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
  systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
}).strict();

export const PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase = z.object({
  name: z.string().optional(),
  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
  apiUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
  token: z.string().optional(),
  username: z.string().optional(),
  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
  allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
  defaultTo: z.string().optional(),
  groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
  groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
  boards: z.record(z.string(), PulseBoardBoardSchema.optional()).optional(),
  textChunkLimit: z.number().int().min(100).max(4000).optional(),
  ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
}).strict();

export const PulseBoardAccountSchema = PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase; // add superRefine for open policy if needed
export const PulseBoardConfigSchema = PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase.extend({
  accounts: z.record(z.string(), PulseBoardAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
});

export const PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseBoardConfigSchema);
```

---

### Step 9: `extensions/pulseboard/src/send.ts`

Outbound message stub. Uses `getPulseBoardRuntime()` for config, resolves account, calls a stub HTTP POST to PulseBoard API.

```typescript
export type SendPulseBoardResult = { messageId: string; target: string };

export async function sendMessagePulseBoard(
  to: string,
  text: string,
  opts: { cfg?: CoreConfig; accountId?: string } = {},
): Promise<SendPulseBoardResult>
```

Implementation: resolves account, validates configured, normalizes target, generates a `messageId`, records activity via `getPulseBoardRuntime().channel.activity.record(...)`. Returns stub result — no real HTTP call (fictional platform).

---

### Step 10: `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-core.ts`

Config patch helpers + `ChannelSetupAdapter`. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts`.

Exported functions:
- `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId, patch)` — wraps `patchScopedAccountConfig`
- `setPulseBoardDmPolicy(cfg, policy)` — wraps `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`
- `setPulseBoardAllowFrom(cfg, allowFrom)` — wraps `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`
- `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter` — validates `token` present, applies `apiUrl` + `token` + `username` fields

---

### Step 11: `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts`

Interactive setup wizard. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts` but simpler (no port/TLS/NickServ).

`pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard` with:
- `channel: "pulseboard"`
- `status` — `createStandardChannelSetupStatus` checking `configured` flag
- `introNote` — title "PulseBoard setup", lists env vars `PULSEBOARD_API_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`
- `prepare` — check env vars for `PULSEBOARD_API_URL` + `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, offer to use them
- `credentials: []`
- `textInputs`:
  - `apiUrl` — PulseBoard API URL (optional, defaults to `https://api.pulseboard.example`)
  - `token` — API token (required)
  - `username` — bot/agent username (required)
  - `groupChannels` — comma-separated board names to monitor (optional)
- `groupAccess` — board policy (`open`/`allowlist`/`disabled`)
- `allowFrom` — `createAllowFromSection` for DM allowlist
- `dmPolicy` — `ircDmPolicy`-style struct
- `finalize` — asks about require-mention for boards
- `completionNote` — next steps
- `disable` — `setSetupChannelEnabled(cfg, "pulseboard", false)`

---

### Step 12: `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts`

Core plugin. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` using `createChatChannelPlugin`.

Key configuration:
- `id: "pulseboard"`
- `capabilities.chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`
- `capabilities.media: false` (stub, no media support)
- `capabilities.blockStreaming: true`
- `outbound.base.textChunkLimit: 4000` (per spec)
- `outbound.base.deliveryMode: "direct"`
- `outbound.attachedResults.sendText` — calls `sendMessagePulseBoard`
- `config` — `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` for multi-account
- `security` — `createScopedDmSecurityResolver` for DM policy
- `groups` — `resolveRequireMention` + `resolveToolPolicy` from board config
- `messaging.normalizeTarget` — `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget`
- `messaging.targetResolver.looksLikeId` — `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId`
- `messaging.targetResolver.hint` — `"<#board|username>"`
- `gateway.startAccount` — `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` (stub: logs and waits for abort)
- `pairing` — text pairing via `sendMessagePulseBoard`
- `status` — `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`
- `directory` — `createChannelDirectoryAdapter` listing peers + boards
- `resolver` — resolves direct/group targets
- `reload.configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"]`

---

### Step 13: `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts`

Main entry. Identical structure to IRC.

```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseBoardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime,
});
```

---

### Step 14: `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts`

```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin);
```

---

### Step 15: `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts`

Public API barrel (setup surface helpers are the main public surface):

```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

### Step 16: `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts`

Basic plugin structure tests. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/channel.test.ts`.

Tests:
1. `pulseBoardPlugin.outbound.chunker` chunks text correctly
2. `pulseBoardPlugin.outbound.textChunkLimit` equals `4000`
3. `pulseBoardPlugin.capabilities.chatTypes` includes both `"direct"` and `"group"`
4. Plugin `id` is `"pulseboard"`

---

## Naming Invariants Satisfied

The `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts` enforces:
1. **Package name** `@openclaw/pulseboard` matches `@openclaw/<id>` ✓
2. **Directory name** `pulseboard` matches `openclaw.plugin.json:id` = `"pulseboard"` ✓
3. **`openclaw.install.npmSpec`** = `"@openclaw/pulseboard"` = `package.json:name` ✓
4. **`openclaw.channel.id`** = `"pulseboard"` = `openclaw.plugin.json:id` ✓

---

## File Creation Order (Dependencies)

1. `openclaw.plugin.json` — standalone
2. `package.json` — standalone
3. `src/types.ts` — no local deps
4. `src/runtime-api.ts` — no local deps (SDK imports only)
5. `src/runtime.ts` — depends on `runtime-api.ts`
6. `src/normalize.ts` — no local deps
7. `src/accounts.ts` — depends on `types.ts`, `runtime-api.ts`
8. `src/config-schema.ts` — no local deps
9. `src/send.ts` — depends on `accounts.ts`, `normalize.ts`, `runtime.ts`, `types.ts`
10. `src/setup-core.ts` — depends on `types.ts`, `normalize.ts`
11. `src/setup-surface.ts` — depends on `accounts.ts`, `normalize.ts`, `setup-core.ts`, `types.ts`
12. `src/channel.ts` — depends on all above
13. `index.ts` — depends on `src/channel.ts`, `src/runtime.ts`
14. `setup-entry.ts` — depends on `src/channel.ts`
15. `api.ts` — depends on `src/accounts.ts`, `src/setup-surface.ts`
16. `src/channel.test.ts` — depends on `src/channel.ts`, `src/runtime.ts`

---

## Verification

1. **Type check** (read-only, non-destructive): `pnpm tsgo` — should pass with no errors in the new files
2. **Test** the new plugin: `pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts`
3. **Plugin naming invariant**: `pnpm test -- src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts`
4. **Lint**: `pnpm check` — should pass with no new lint errors
5. **Manual inspection**: verify `defineChannelPluginEntry` export default in `index.ts` resolves correctly

file.content:
Here's a summary of my research and the plan I've built:

---

## Plan Summary: PulseBoard Channel Plugin

### What I found

After deeply exploring the IRC, Nostr, and Telegram channel plugins, plus the Plugin SDK contracts, I have a complete picture of the extension structure. All bundled channel plugins under `extensions/<id>/` follow the same consistent pattern.

### Files to create (16 total, all under `extensions/pulseboard/`)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Manifest: `id="pulseboard"`, `channels=["pulseboard"]` |
| `package.json` | `@openclaw/pulseboard`, version, `openclaw.channel.id`, labels, etc. |
| `index.ts` | `defineChannelPluginEntry` — full runtime entry |
| `setup-entry.ts` | `defineSetupPluginEntry` — setup-only entry |
| `api.ts` | Public barrel re-exporting accounts + setup surface |
| `src/types.ts` | `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`, `PulseBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage` |
| `src/runtime-api.ts` | Internal barrel re-exporting generic helpers from SDK |
| `src/runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` — thin store for `getPulseBoardRuntime` / `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseBoardAccount`, `listPulseBoardAccountIds`, env var fallbacks |
| `src/normalize.ts` | `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget`, `isBoardTarget`, allow-entry normalization |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema via `buildChannelConfigSchema` with DM + group policy fields |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseBoard` stub (no real network — fictional platform) |
| `src/setup-core.ts` | `ChannelSetupAdapter` + patch helpers (`updatePulseBoardAccountConfig`, etc.) |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `ChannelSetupWizard` — prompts for API URL, token, username, boards |
| `src/channel.ts` | `createChatChannelPlugin` — capabilities `["direct","group"]`, chunk limit 4000 |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | Tests: chunking, textChunkLimit=4000, chatTypes, plugin id |

### Key design decisions
- **chatTypes:** `["direct", "group"]` — both DMs and named boards
- **textChunkLimit:** 4000 characters per spec
- **Boards** use `#board-name` prefix (like IRC channels); DMs use bare usernames
- **Multi-account support** via `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` (same as IRC)
- **Naming invariants** satisfied: directory = id = channel.id = npmSpec stem = `pulseboard`
- **No existing files modified**

15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,

77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',

92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95

// Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmConfig,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig,
  GroupToolPolicyConfig,
  MarkdownConfig,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

content.thinking:
Good. Now I see that there's an `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` facade that the IRC plugin's `runtime-api.ts` uses. For PulseBoard, I can't re-use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` because that's IRC-specific. Let me check what's in the core channel SDK that I can use more generically.

Let me look at what's exported from a few key SDK files that I'll need for PulseBoard:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` - core createChatChannelPlugin, defineChannelPluginEntry, etc.
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` - buildChannelConfigSchema, etc.


- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store` - createPluginRuntimeStore
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` - createAccountListHelpers, describeAccountSnapshot
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` - DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID, normalizeAccountId
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution` - resolveMergedAccountConfig
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input` - normalizeResolvedSecretInputString
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` - createScopedChannelConfigAdapter, adaptScopedAccountAccessor

, createScopedDmSecurityResolver
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` - composeAccountWarningCollectors, createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` - createComputedAccountStatusAdapter, createDefaultChannelRuntimeState
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime` - createChannelDirectoryAdapter, createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from` - formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` - various setup helpers
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` - normalizeAccountId
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared` - runStoppablePassiveMonitor

Now I need to figure out what the IRC plugin's `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` module exports, since the runtime API is re-exporting from it.

Looking at the IRC module, it exports a bunch of functions for managing channel status, handling text delivery, controlling pairings, and enforcing policies around allowlists and group policies, along with some constants and type definitions.

All 16 files created, naming invariants satisfied, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and `chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`. Here's a summary of everything that was implemented:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Complete

All 16 files were created under `extensions/pulseboard/`. No existing files were modified.

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Manifest: `id="pulseboard"`, `channels=["pulseboard"]` |
| `package.json` | `@openclaw/pulseboard`, channel metadata, `npmSpec`, `setupEntry` |
| `index.ts` | `defineChannelPluginEntry` wrapping `pulseBoardPlugin` + `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `setup-entry.ts` | `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |
| `api.ts` | Public barrel re-exporting accounts + setup surface |
| `src/types.ts` | `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `PulseBoardBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, `PulseBoardProbe`; local type aliases for config types not in public SDK subpaths |
| `src/runtime-api.ts` | Imports from individual public SDK subpaths; implements `chunkTextForOutbound` locally (no dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` facade exists) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>` → `get/set/clearPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseBoardAccount`, `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts`; env vars `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME` |
| `src/normalize.ts` | `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget`, `isBoardTarget` (`#board-name` prefix); allow-entry normalization and allowlist matching |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema via `buildChannelConfigSchema`; validates `dmPolicy="open"` requires `allowFrom` with `"*"` |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseBoard` stub — fictional platform, records outbound activity, no real network call |
| `src/setup-core.ts` | `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig`, `setPulseBoardDmPolicy`, `setPulseBoardAllowFrom`, `setPulseBoardBoardAccess`, `pulseBoardSetupAdapter` |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `pulseBoardSetupWizard` — prompts for API URL, token, username, boards; env-var detection; DM policy |
| `src/channel.ts` | `pulseBoardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin`; meta defined inline (not via `getChatChannelMeta` since `"pulseboard"` is not in `ChatChannelId`); `capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct","group"] }`, `textChunkLimit: 4000` |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | Tests: plugin id, chatTypes, textChunkLimit=4000, chunker word-boundary splitting, short-text pass-through |

### Key design decisions

- **`textChunkLimit: 4000`** — per spec; boards use `#board-name`, DMs use bare usernames
- **`chatTypes: ["direct", "group"]`** — both DMs and named boards supported
- **`chunkTextForOutbound` implemented locally** — no public SDK subpath exposes this; mirrors the shared chunker logic exactly
- **Meta defined inline** — `"pulseboard"` is not in the core `ChatChannelId` union, so meta follows the mattermost pattern (`const meta = { ... } as const`)
- **Multi-account** via `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, same as IRC
- **All naming invariants satisfied**: directory = `id` = `channel.id` = npm package stem = `pulseboard`
